analysis.semantic_summary: This parser processes Microsoft 365 Defender security events, specifically focusing on process activity and system events. It transforms raw Microsoft Defender logs into standardized OCSF format, capturing process launches, file operations, network connections, and security detections with associated metadata like timestamps, device information, and user context.
optimization_opportunities: Implement error handling for timestamp conversion
analysis.lua_code:
-- Microsoft 365 Defender Parser for Observo.ai
-- OCSF Class: Process Activity (1007) 
-- Optimized for 10k+ events/sec
-- Generated: 2025-10-13

-- Pre-declare locals for performance
local type = type
local tonumber = tonumber
local os_time = os.time
local string_format = string.format

-- Validation helper functions
local function validate_string(str)
    return type(str) == "string" and str ~= ""
end

local function validate_number(num)
    return type(num) == "number" and num > 0
end

-- ISO8601 to epoch converter
local function iso8601_to_epoch(timestamp)
    if not validate_string(timestamp) then return nil end
    -- Basic ISO8601 parsing - enhance as needed
    local year, month, day, hour, min, sec = timestamp:match("(%d+)-(%d+)-(%d+)T(%d+):(%d+):(%d+)")
    if not year then return nil end
    return os.time({year=year, month=month, day=day, hour=hour, min=min, sec=sec})
end

function transform(record)
    -- Input validation
    if not record or type(record) ~= "table" then
        return nil, "Invalid input record"
    end

    -- Initialize output with constant fields
    local output = {
        activity_id = 1,
        activity_name = "Launch",
        category_uid = 1,
        category_name = "System Activity",
        class_uid = 1007,
        class_name = "Process Activity",
        type_uid = 100701,
        type_name = "Process Activity: Launch"
    }

    -- Timestamp handling with validation
    if record.Timestamp then
        local epoch = iso8601_to_epoch(record.Timestamp)
        if epoch then
            output.time = epoch
        else
            output.time = os_time()
        end
    end

    -- Device information
    if validate_string(record.DeviceName) then
        output.device = output.device or {}
        output.device.hostname = record.DeviceName
    end
    
    if validate_string(record.DeviceId) then
        output.device = output.device or {}
        output.device.uid = record.DeviceId
    end

    -- Actor information
    if validate_string(record.AccountName) or validate_string(record.AccountDomain) then
        output.actor = {
            user = {
                name = record.AccountName,
                domain = record.AccountDomain
            }
        }
    end

    -- Process information
    if validate_string(record.ProcessName) or validate_string(record.ProcessCommandLine) then
        output.process = {
            name = record.ProcessName,
            command_line = record.ProcessCommandLine,
            pid = tonumber(record.ProcessId)
        }
        
        -- Parent process info
        if validate_string(record.ParentProcessName) or validate_number(record.ParentProcessId) then
            output.process.parent_process = {
                name = record.ParentProcessName,
                pid = tonumber(record.ParentProcessId)
            }
        end
    end

    -- File information
    if validate_string(record.FileName) or validate_string(record.FolderPath) then
        output.file = {
            name = record.FileName,
            path = record.FolderPath,
            hashes = {
                sha1 = record.SHA1,
                md5 = record.MD5
            }
        }
    end

    -- Network information
    if validate_string(record.RemoteIP) or validate_number(record.RemotePort) then
        output.dst_endpoint = {
            ip = record.RemoteIP,
            port = tonumber(record.RemotePort),
            url = record.RemoteUrl and {url_string = record.RemoteUrl} or nil
        }
    end

    -- Connection info
    if validate_string(record.Protocol) then
        output.connection_info = {
            protocol_name = record.Protocol
        }
    end

    -- Final validation
    if not output.time then
        output.time = os_time()
    end

    return output
end
monitoring_recommendations: Track average processing time per event
